summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting featuring the swearing-in of two new firefighter/paramedics (Jerry Burns and Ryan Petronio), addressed multiple personnel matters including resignations and new hires, approved the purchase of a station vehicle, accepted a donation and a grant, scheduled a zoning workshop, and delivered a detailed public rebuttal to allegations made by county commissioner candidate Logan Bixler.
decisions: Motion to recess for light refreshments passed
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Brody Green effective March 12, 2025 passed
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Thomas Jetter effective March 12, 2025 passed
Motion to purchase 2023 Ford F250 from Mount Ward Ford for $46,000 passed
Motion to accept $5,000 donation to Police Department from Day Heights Memorial Firefighters Association passed
Motion to accept $5,000 grant from Duke Energy Foundation for weather siren passed
Motion to schedule Board of Trustees special meeting/zoning workshop for April 3, 2025 at 6pm passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 6:45pm passed
Motion to hire Paul Hilliard as part-time fitness trainer at $30/hour effective May 4, 2025 passed
Motion to laterally hire full-time firefighter paramedic Jameson Benoit at step three of CBA with one-year probation effective April 13, 2025 passed
action_items: Chief Meyer to oversee Station 18 foundation digging starting week of April 7
John Langenheim (new Planning and Zoning Director) to prepare for zoning workshop on April 3, 2025
Invite Zoning Commission, BZA, and Attorney Kevin Maloff to April 3 zoning workshop
Fire department to outfit purchased 2023 Ford F250 with cap, hose roller from BWC, and prepare for towing duties
Process hire of Paul Hilliard as part-time fitness trainer effective May 4, 2025
Process lateral hire of Jameson Benoit as full-time firefighter paramedic effective April 13, 2025
key_amounts: $46,000 for purchase of 2023 Ford F250 station vehicle (budgeted at $50,000)
$5,000 donation from Day Heights Memorial Firefighters Association to Police Department
$5,000 grant from Duke Energy Foundation for weather siren
$30/hour rate for part-time fitness trainer Paul Hilliard
Nearly $7 million secured in federal, state, and county funds for Township projects
$25,000 additional salary for Administrator Rose's expanded dual role
$1,000 one-time suit allowance over five-year contract for Administrator Rose
notable_topics: Swearing-in ceremony for firefighter/paramedics Jerry Burns and Ryan Petronio
Fire Station 18 construction progress update - foundation digging scheduled for week of April 7
New T-66 equipment delivered for road department
Personnel resignations of part-time firefighters Brody Green and Thomas Jetter
Purchase of 2023 Ford F250 station vehicle
Detailed public rebuttal by Administrator Rose to allegations by county commissioner candidate Logan Bixler
Scheduling of zoning workshop special meeting for April 3, 2025
Lateral hire of full-time firefighter paramedic Jameson Benoit
Hire of part-time fitness trainer Paul Hilliard
attendees: John Langenheim (new Planning and Zoning Director)
notes: Trustee Allen was absent; the invocation requested prayers for Allen's 'traveling mercies' during a 'time of unknown.' A significant portion of the meeting was devoted to Administrator Rose's detailed point-by-point rebuttal of allegations made by Logan Bixler, a county commissioner candidate, who had spoken at the previous March 11 meeting and posted on Facebook. Rose cited Ohio Revised Code 505.032, a 2005 Ohio Ethics Commission letter, and a BCI investigation summary clearing him and Sergeant Hampton to refute claims. The meeting also included an executive session. The visit to Clear Creek fire station by Conger was referenced, noting their $12 million station plus $5 million property cost.
title: BOT 3-25-25
